tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mildly elevated serum creatinine, low total CO₂, and significant proteinuria with low urine specific gravity, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) may indicate urinary tract pathology, though nitrites and leukocytes are negative. Low HbA1c is unusual and may reflect anemia or lab error. Recommend further renal evaluation and clinical correlation for possible chronic kidney disease and urinary tract issues.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
